title: New photo released in unsolved 1997 homicide of a N.H. woman
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A newly released photo aims to find witnesses in the unsolved 1997 homicide of Rosalie Miller in Auburn, NH.
Rosalie Miller, 36, was last seen in Manchester on December 8, 1996, and found dead on January 20, 1997.
Authorities urge anyone with information about Miller's case to contact the New Hampshire Cold Case Unit.

title: Man allegedly kills man in R.I. before causing car crash that killed 2 in Mass.
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Demitri Sousa, 28, allegedly killed Javon Lawson in Cranston, R.I., before causing a fatal crash in Swansea, Mass.
Sousa faces charges of murder, firearm use during a crime, and carrying an unlicensed pistol, Cranston Police said.
The crash involved Sousa's Infiniti and a Subaru, killing two. Sousa is hospitalized with serious injuries, police said.

title: Weighing the pros and cons of a Robert Thomas trade for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins may trade for St. Louis Blues center Robert Thomas, requiring significant draft picks and prospects.
Robert Thomas, 26, has surpassed 80 points in each of the last two NHL seasons, making him a valuable target.
St. Louis is not under pressure to trade Thomas immediately, potentially prolonging negotiations with interested teams.

title: An update: Did a Brooklyn couple kill a neighbor’s trees for a better view in Maine?
author: New York Times Service
bullets: Stephen Antonson and Kathleen Hackett settled with Maine for $3,000 over tree poisoning allegations without admitting guilt.
The pesticide board's apology to the victim's family highlights dissatisfaction with the settlement's lack of accountability.
Victim Ruth Graham's family urged the couple to contribute to Maine coastal preservation and publicly address the incident.

title: 10 combine standouts that could make sense for Patriots to select in April’s NFL Draft
author: Conor Roche
bullets: The Patriots are considering 10 NFL draft prospects after notable performances at the 2026 NFL Scouting Combine.
Edge rushers Romello Height, Dani Dennis-Sutton, and Malachi Lawrence could be potential Day 2 picks for New England.
Wide receiver KC Concepcion impressed with strong route-running, while TE Eli Stowers set a vertical jump record.

title: Insider: Patriots probably won’t make a ‘Micah Parsons-type’ trade this offseason
author: Conor Roche
bullets: The Patriots are unlikely to trade significant draft capital for star players this offseason, focusing on long-term team building instead.
Potential trades for Eagles' A.J. Brown and Raiders' Maxx Crosby would require a substantial offer similar to what Cowboys received for Micah Parsons.
The Patriots have 11 draft picks in 2026, with $40 million in cap space, but are not expected to make major moves in free agency.

title: Largest energy storage facility statewide gets approved for construction in Everett
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Jupiter Power receives state approval to build a major energy storage system at the old ExxonMobil tank farm in Everett.
The facility will feature 816 lithium-ion battery enclosures and support Massachusetts' clean energy goals by storing excess grid power.
The Everett project is part of the 'Docklands' development, combining energy storage with commercial and residential spaces.

title: ‘SNL’ recap: Best moments from Connor Storrie hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Connor Storrie hosted 'SNL' on Feb. 28 with musical guest Mumford & Sons for the show's 51st season return.
Storrie and 'Heated Rivalry' co-star Hudson Williams reunited in sketches and introduced Mumford & Sons on 'SNL'.
U.S. Olympic hockey athletes appeared in the monologue, making jokes about President Trump's congratulatory comments to the teams.

title: 3 takeaways from the Revolution’s loss against the Red Bulls
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The Revolution lost 1-0 to the New York Red Bulls at Sports Illustrated Stadium, starting the season with two consecutive defeats.
Limited chances were created as the Revolution struggled with the Red Bulls' press, controlling only 27% of first-half possession.
A key second-half opportunity was missed by Jackson Yueill and Griffin Yow, failing to capitalize on the match's best chance.

title: Triston Casas reportedly unlikely to be ready by Opening Day, while Kutter Crawford, Patrick Sandoval take step forward in rehab
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Triston Casas is unlikely to be ready for MLB action by Opening Day due to a torn patellar tendon.
Kutter Crawford and Patrick Sandoval are progressing in their rehabilitation and recently pitched live batting practice.
Both Crawford and Sandoval are uncertain about making the Opening Day roster but are optimistic about joining the team soon.
